page: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which provides 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, the instance integrates four NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 179 GB of VRAM.

The instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and supports a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the g6e.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance within the top 10% for large language model inference, specifically in prompt processing and text generation across 135M, 7B, and 70B parameter models. It also registers strong performance in stress-ng CPU tests, Passmark CPU and memory evaluations, and Geekbench ray tracing and image processing suites, while maintaining average performance in standard database operations and large-block memory bandwidth.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g6e.12xlarge provides a dense consolidation of high-performance GPU accelerators, local NVMe storage, and dedicated CPU resources, reducing the need for multi-node overhead in complex deployments. Based on these hardware specifications and benchmark results, this server is optimized for large language model inference, graphics-intensive rendering, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput machine learning workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 vCPUs from a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM, a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in LLM inference for prompt processing and text generation, alongside strong results in ray tracing and image processing. This instance is optimized for large language model deployment, rendering, and high-performance computing tasks requiring dense GPU acceleration and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB VRAM, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance AI and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring four NVIDIA L40S accelerators and local NVMe storage for advanced AI inference and rendering.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs providing 179 GB of total VRAM
48 vCPUs powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.4 GHz
384 GB DDR4 system memory and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference and prompt processing
Suited for machine learning deployment, ray tracing, and graphics rendering
